title: antiaris toxicaria subsp. macrophylla
intro: Antiaris toxicaria subsp. macrophylla, also known as the Kukui tree or Canoe tree, is a plant species in the family Moraceae, native to the Pacific Islands, particularly in Hawaii. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Antiaris toxicaria subsp. macrophylla include the potential treatment of skin conditions, wounds, and infections, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy through various preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
